root_text.iti85:2.2: ānāpānassati ca vo ajjhattaṁ parimukhaṁ sūpaṭṭhitā hotu;
root_text.iti85:2.4: Asubhānupassīnaṁ, bhikkhave, kāyasmiṁ viharataṁ yo subhāya dhātuyā rāgānusayo so pahīyati.
root_text.iti85:2.5: Ānāpānassatiyā ajjhattaṁ parimukhaṁ sūpaṭṭhititāya ye bāhirā vitakkāsayā vighātapakkhikā, te na honti.
root_text.iti85:2.6: Sabbasaṅkhāresu aniccānupassīnaṁ viharataṁ yā avijjā sā pahīyati, yā vijjā sā uppajjatī”ti.
root_text.iti85:5.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti85:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti85:2.1: “Mendicants, meditate observing the ugliness of the body.
translation_text.iti85:2.2: Let mindfulness of breathing be well-established internally in your presence.
translation_text.iti85:2.3: Meditate observing the impermanence of all conditions.
translation_text.iti85:2.4: As you meditate observing the ugliness of the body, you will give up desire for the body.
translation_text.iti85:2.5: When mindfulness of breathing is well-established internally in your presence, there will be no distressing external thoughts or wishes.
translation_text.iti85:2.6: When you meditate observing the impermanence of all conditions, ignorance is given up and knowledge arises.”
translation_text.iti85:4.2: and when freed in regards to that,
translation_text.iti85:4.3: that peaceful sage, with perfect insight,
translation_text.iti85:5.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
